pattern: Since only one element may ever be dropped, precomputed prefix and suffix GCD/LCM tables let every 'remove element i' scenario be answered in constant time instead of recomputing from scratch.
hints: The factor score of the whole array is one candidate answer; every other candidate comes from dropping exactly one element, so you need the GCD and LCM of 'everything except index i' for every i.

Build prefix and suffix arrays of running GCD and running LCM across the array; combining the prefix up to i-1 with the suffix from i+1 gives the GCD and LCM of the array with index i removed, without rescanning it.

Take the maximum of the full array's factor score and every one-element-removed factor score computed from those prefix/suffix tables; a length-one array's only real option is keeping its single element, which squares it.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, removing the odd one out doubles the score
statement example, keeping the whole array is already optimal
statement example, a single element squares itself
two elements at the maximum value
removing the smallest value does not help since it doesn't lower the GCD
coprime values where the full array already has GCD 1

stress.description: random small-valued arrays exercising many different removal scenarios
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
from math import gcd


class Solution:
    def maxScore(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        prefix_gcd = [0] * (n + 1)
        suffix_gcd = [0] * (n + 1)
        prefix_lcm = [1] * (n + 1)
        suffix_lcm = [1] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n):
            prefix_gcd[i + 1] = gcd(prefix_gcd[i], nums[i])
            prefix_lcm[i + 1] = prefix_lcm[i] * nums[i] // gcd(prefix_lcm[i], nums[i])
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            suffix_gcd[i] = gcd(suffix_gcd[i + 1], nums[i])
            suffix_lcm[i] = suffix_lcm[i + 1] * nums[i] // gcd(suffix_lcm[i + 1], nums[i])
        best = prefix_gcd[n] * prefix_lcm[n]
        for i in range(n):
            g = gcd(prefix_gcd[i], suffix_gcd[i + 1])
            l = prefix_lcm[i] * suffix_lcm[i + 1] // gcd(prefix_lcm[i], suffix_lcm[i + 1])
            score = g * l
            if score > best:
                best = score
        return best
